meta.title: PyTorch Mastery Plan
meta.goal: Complete Learn PyTorch chapters 01-09, then a capstone, before the UT Austin MSAI deep learning course (starts 2026-08-25).

chapters.title: Experiment Tracking
milestones: TensorBoard runs compared, best model chosen

chapters.title: Model Deployment
milestones: FoodVision demo deployed to HF Spaces

chapters.title: Capstone & Course Prep
milestones: Ready for UT Austin DL course (Aug 25)

tasks.description: Read 01 from the intro through 'Build model'. Create synthetic linear data y = weight*X + bias, do a train/test split, and plot it. Build LinearRegressionModel(nn.Module) with nn.Parameter and forward(). Inspect .parameters() and .state_dict().

From memory: pick L1Loss and SGD optimizer, then write the 5 steps cold: forward pass, loss, optimizer.zero_grad(), loss.backward(), optimizer.step(). Train 100+ epochs, track the loss, evaluate inside torch.inference_mode(). This loop is the single most important thing in the course.
tasks.deliverable: Training loop typed from memory in exercise notebook

tasks.description: Save and load the model with state_dict (torch.save / torch.load). Make the whole workflow device-agnostic. Then rebuild the entire workflow in a blank cell with no reference. Do the chapter exercises.

Make the make_circles dataset. Build a purely linear model and watch it fail on non-linear data. Use BCEWithLogitsLoss, apply sigmoid to get prediction probabilities, and introduce nn.ReLU to add non-linearity.

From memory: model with nn.Linear + nn.ReLU layers, accuracy function, and the train/eval loop. Plot the decision boundary with plot_decision_boundary and confirm the non-linear model separates the circles.
tasks.deliverable: Classifier training loop in exercise notebook

tasks.description: make_blobs multiclass problem with softmax + CrossEntropyLoss. Build an accuracy metric and understand the confusion-matrix idea. Do the chapter exercises.
Load torchvision.datasets.FashionMNIST, visualize samples, and batch it with DataLoader. Build baseline model 0: flatten + nn.Linear. Understand why a flat model ignores spatial structure.

Build TinyVGG: two conv blocks (Conv2d -> ReLU -> Conv2d -> ReLU -> MaxPool2d) then a classifier. Reason carefully about in/out channels, kernel_size, stride, and padding, and how each changes the tensor shape.
tasks.deliverable: TinyVGG model coded in exercise notebook

tasks.description: Factor out train_step() and test_step(). Train TinyVGG on FashionMNIST. Build a confusion matrix and plot a grid of predictions with their probabilities.
Compare model 0 vs the CNN on accuracy, loss, and training time. Time CPU vs GPU. Use torchinfo.summary to inspect parameter counts. Do the chapter exercises.

Download the pizza/steak/sushi data and explore its folder structure. Compose torchvision transforms (Resize, ToTensor). Load it with datasets.ImageFolder + DataLoader and visualize transformed images.
Build ImageFolderCustom(Dataset) from memory: __init__, __len__, __getitem__, and a class-to-index mapping. Verify it returns the same samples as torchvision's ImageFolder.
tasks.deliverable: Custom Dataset subclass in exercise notebook

tasks.description: Add data augmentation (TrivialAugmentWide, RandomHorizontalFlip). Train TinyVGG on the custom data both with and without augmentation and compare.
Plot train/test loss curves and diagnose overfitting vs underfitting. Predict on a single custom image end-to-end. Do the chapter exercises.
Convert the Ch 04 notebook into a going_modular package: data_setup.py (create_dataloaders), model_builder.py (TinyVGG), engine.py (train_step/test_step/train).
Add utils.py (save_model) and a train.py entry point that reads hyperparameters via argparse. Run the whole training end-to-end from the terminal, not a notebook. Do the exercises.
tasks.deliverable: Run training from CLI; finish ch05_modular

tasks.description: Load torchvision.models EfficientNet_B0 with its weights and auto-transforms. Freeze the base layers (requires_grad=False) and replace the classifier head for 3 classes.
Train only the new classifier head on pizza/steak/sushi, reusing engine.train(). Watch it hit high accuracy in very few epochs compared with training from scratch.
Compare scratch TinyVGG vs transfer learning on accuracy and training time. Use pred_and_plot_image on your own custom images. Do the chapter exercises.
Set up torch.utils.tensorboard.SummaryWriter and log loss/accuracy scalars. Write a create_writer() helper that names runs by experiment, model, and timestamp.
Loop over combinations: EffNetB0 vs B2, 10% vs 20% data, 5 vs 10 epochs. Save each run with its own named writer so they are comparable.
Open TensorBoard, compare the runs visually, choose the best model, save it, and predict with it. Do the chapter exercises.
Read 'An Image is Worth 16x16 Words': Figure 1 and Equations 1-4. Map every paper term to a PyTorch layer. Get the pizza/steak/sushi data ready as the testbed.
Turn an image into patch embeddings with nn.Conv2d + nn.Flatten (PatchEmbedding). Prepend a learnable class token and add learnable position embeddings. This is Equation 1.
Build MultiheadSelfAttentionBlock (LayerNorm + nn.MultiheadAttention) and MLPBlock (LayerNorm + MLP). These are Equations 2 and 3 of the paper.
Combine the blocks into a TransformerEncoderBlock with residual connections, stack N of them, add the classifier head (Equation 4), and train the ViT from scratch on 3 classes.
Load torchvision's pretrained ViT_B_16, feature-extract on the same data, and compare it against your from-scratch ViT. Do the chapter exercises.
Think through deployment questions (where, how fast, how big). Prepare FoodVision Mini (EffNetB2 feature extractor) and export it. Learn the basics of Gradio.
Write a predict() function and wrap the model in a Gradio Interface running locally. Test it on example images and measure per-prediction latency.
Scale up to the larger Food101 problem with an EffNetB2 / ViT feature extractor. Gather results and reason about the accuracy vs speed vs size tradeoff.
Package app.py, the model, and requirements, then deploy the demo to Hugging Face Spaces. Write the demo README. Do the exercises. learnpytorch.io 01-09 is now COMPLETE.
tasks.deliverable: Live HF Spaces demo; finish ch09_model_deployment

tasks.description: Pick an end-to-end project: your own image dataset -> transfer-learned model -> deployed demo. Write a one-paragraph spec and a single success metric.
Gather and clean the data. Build the Dataset, transforms, and DataLoaders for it.
Transfer-learn an EfficientNet/ViT backbone, write the training loop, and get a baseline result.
Run TensorBoard experiments, tune, and pick the best model. Save it. (Aug 10 is a flex day for overflow.)
Build the Gradio app and push it to Hugging Face Spaces.
Write the project README with results and screenshots. Clean up all chapter READMEs. (Aug 12 and 14 are flex days.)
From a blank notebook, rebuild from memory: the training loop, a CNN, a custom Dataset, transfer learning, and TensorBoard logging. Note every weak spot.
Skim the UT Austin deep learning course outline (ut.philkr.net/deeplearning). Set up the course environment: conda/uv, CUDA, repo. (Aug 17 and 19 are flex days.)
Build a one-page reference: tensor ops, the 5-step training loop, core nn modules, and reshape/einops patterns. Drill tensor shapes until automatic.
Tag a release per chapter, make sure every notebook runs clean top-to-bottom, and push the final repo. (Aug 21 is a flex day.)
Final cold review of your weak-spot list. PyTorch prep is complete. The UT Austin deep learning course starts tomorrow.
